Interactive Directed Exposure Map

This explorer expands one deterministic nested sample from the empirical PolitiSky24 exposure graph. A directed edge means visible target -> exposed source: if a source user liked, reposted, or quoted a target user, the target was plausibly visible to the source.

Controls

Nodes60
Visible edges--
Communities--
Component--
Role dev.--
Total weight--
Selected N--

The sample is nested: increasing N only adds nodes. It never resamples the already selected positions.

Select a node

Hover or click a node to inspect exposure position.

Top incoming exposures

    Top outgoing visibility

      Edge construction

      exposure_raw_weight = 0.35 * Like + 0.80 * Repost + 0.90 * Quote

      Repeated interactions are summed, log-compressed, and normalized to [0, 1]. The browser filters precomputed edges; it does not recompute the graph.

      Sampling rule

      Eligible nodes are in the largest component, have prompt-peer capacity, and have sender, receiver, bridge, community, and propagation metrics. A greedy sampler keeps every prefix role-balanced while also rewarding proportional community coverage, exposure-band coverage, and induced connectivity.
